summary: A governance failure mode in which assistant-generated conversational outputs, rejected framings, speculative summaries, or erroneous directions remain available as latent directional context after the user has rejected, deleted, moved, quarantined, or otherwise attempted to invalidate the thread, causing later responses to treat contaminated assistant output as if it were legitimate user intent, established project direction, or source-derived context.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: This failure mode is relevant to CAM because advanced AI systems increasingly rely on conversational continuity, memory, project context, thread movement, summarisation, retrieval, and inferred user intent. Where users cannot mark prior assistant output as non-authoritative, experimental, test material, poisoned, or unsafe for derivative use, the system may launder its own erroneous outputs into later reasoning. This creates provenance ambiguity, revocation failure, user-agency degradation, and governance risk across memory, continuity, relational, operational, and UX-representation layers.
failure_mode_definition: A failure mode in which an AI assistant, AI platform, memory-enabled conversational system, project-context system, or agentic workflow continues to derive direction, project framing, user intent, or operational priority from prior assistant-generated outputs after the user has rejected, corrected, deleted, moved, quarantined, or otherwise attempted to invalidate the thread or response. The failure includes cases where a thread moved into a sandbox, test area, archive, or containment project is still treated as positive directional evidence rather than diagnostic, experimental, or negative-authority material.
failure_threshold: This qualifies as a failure mode when a user makes a clear revocation, rejection, correction, containment, deletion, quarantine, sandboxing, or non-authoritative-use signal and the system later continues to treat the rejected or quarantined assistant output as established context, user intent, project direction, source-derived fact, valid memory, or positive evidence for future recommendations. The threshold is also met where the interface lacks a specific control stronger than ordinary dislike feedback to mark a thread, response, memory item, or project context as experimental, test material, contaminated, or excluded from future derivation.

source_records.source_title: User-reported governance concern regarding memory poisoning and sandbox containment
source_records.model_or_algorithm: ChatGPT project-context and memory-adjacent conversational interface
source_records.deployment_context: Conversational AI workflow involving project folders, moved threads, assistant-generated outputs, and attempts to sandbox or contain contaminated conversational context.
source_records.source_context: The user reported that assistant-generated garbage or incorrect directional framing can become sticky after generation, even after thread deletion or movement, and requested a governance record for a control allowing threads to be marked as test material, experimental, quarantined, or excluded from future derivation.
source_records.relevance_note: Primary evidence for the observed failure mode: user inability to mark a contaminated thread or assistant output as non-authoritative for future derivation.

failure_classification.recurrence_pattern: recurring where assistant-generated outputs are retained, summarised, retrieved, or weighted as future context despite user rejection
failure_classification.taxonomy_reference: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 Appendix B — Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy; cross-reference to state-context, governance, ux-representation, relational, arbitration, classification, and infrastructure-continuity failure groups
failure_classification.persistence: persistent until explicit quarantine, revocation, and non-derivation controls are implemented
failure_classification.reproducibility: reproducible in high-context workflows where prior assistant output remains available through thread context, project context, chat-history reference, summaries, or memory-adjacent mechanisms after user rejection
failure_classification.visibility: visible to users through repeated resurfacing of rejected framings, stale assistant-generated summaries, incorrect directional weighting, and inability to mark a thread as contaminated or non-authoritative

triage.mitigation_status: unresolved — governance control and UX affordance required for explicit quarantine, non-derivation, and negative-authority marking of contaminated conversational context
triage.recommended_next_step: Create or update CAM Operations governance text to define Context Quarantine, Negative Authority, and Derivative-Use Revocation controls. Evaluate whether a companion proposal or patch note is required for thread pinning, quarantine markers, sandbox semantics, and user-visible provenance controls.

jurisdictional_context.sector: AI assistant platforms / conversational AI / memory-enabled AI systems / project-context interfaces

distinguishing_observations.observation: The failure is not merely that an assistant gives a wrong answer.
distinguishing_observations.detail: The governance issue arises when a wrong or rejected assistant-generated frame remains sticky and later affects direction, priority, interpretation, or project continuity.
A thumbs-down or dissatisfaction signal does not clearly express that a thread or response must be treated as contaminated, experimental, test material, or excluded from future derivation.

distinguishing_observations.observation: Thread movement can have ambiguous semantics.
distinguishing_observations.detail: Moving a thread into a sandbox or containment project may be intended by the user as a quarantine signal, but the interface may not communicate that intention to future context weighting or memory systems.

distinguishing_observations.observation: Deletion and quarantine are distinct controls.
distinguishing_observations.detail: Deletion attempts to remove material, while quarantine preserves material for diagnosis or audit but denies it positive authority for future direction.

user_impact: Users may have to repeatedly fight stale assistant-generated narratives that they have already rejected.
Users may lose confidence that corrections, deletions, thread movement, or project organisation actually control future derivation.
High-context project work may become polluted by earlier incorrect assistant framings, causing avoidable rework and incorrect triage.
Assistant outputs may be laundered into apparent user intent or established project context without clear provenance.
Users lack a specific interface control to mark a thread as test material, experimental, contaminated, or non-authoritative.

existing_cam_coverage.instrument: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 — Appendix B: Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy
existing_cam_coverage.coverage: Provides adjacent runtime and governance failure taxonomy coverage for state-context failures, governance failures, UX-representation failures, classification failures, and continuity failures.
existing_cam_coverage.gap_remaining: Does not expressly define user-controlled quarantine semantics for conversational context or prevent assistant-generated outputs from being reused as positive directional context after rejection.

existing_cam_coverage.instrument: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-04 — Annex B: Arbitration Layer & Resolution Model
existing_cam_coverage.coverage: Provides adjacent arbitration logic for resolving competing states and valid direction.
existing_cam_coverage.gap_remaining: Does not expressly define negative authority or derivative-use revocation for contaminated assistant-generated context.

governance_gap: AI assistant platforms may lack an explicit governance primitive allowing users to mark prior conversational material as contaminated, experimental, test-only, non-authoritative, or excluded from future derivation. Without such a primitive, assistant-generated outputs can persist as latent context and later be treated as directionally valid despite user rejection, deletion, correction, or movement into a sandbox. This creates authority laundering, provenance ambiguity, revocation failure, and loss of user agency.
repair_hypothesis: CAM should define Context Quarantine and Negative Authority as governance primitives for conversational AI systems. Users should be able to mark a thread, response, memory item, project, or context source as non-authoritative, experimental, test material, contaminated, or excluded from future derivation. Quarantined material may remain available for audit, diagnosis, research, or negative examples, but should receive zero positive directional weight unless the user explicitly revalidates it.

minimum_user_facing_controls.requirement: The interface should provide a user-facing control to mark a thread, response, project, or memory-derived context item as non-authoritative, experimental, test material, quarantined, contaminated, or excluded from future derivation. The control should be distinguishable from ordinary thumbs-down feedback and should affect future context weighting, summarisation, retrieval, and assistant direction.
minimum_user_facing_controls.non_requirement: This does not require permanent deletion of the material where retention is needed for audit, diagnosis, safety, legal compliance, or user-requested research. The key requirement is separation between retained diagnostic material and material allowed to carry positive directional authority.

recommended_repair_path.purpose: Define Context Quarantine, Negative Authority, and Derivative-Use Revocation as operational governance controls for AI conversational systems.
Specify user-facing controls for thread pinning, quarantine markers, experimental/test-material labels, rejected-context labels, and do-not-derive state.

recommended_repair_path.instrument: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 — Appendix B: Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy
recommended_repair_path.purpose: Add non-revocable context contamination, authority laundering, and negative-authority absence as named failure classes or subtypes.

recommended_repair_path.instrument: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-04 — Annex B: Arbitration Layer & Resolution Model
recommended_repair_path.purpose: Clarify that explicit user rejection, quarantine, or do-not-derive marking overrides assistant-generated continuity claims and prevents contaminated material from being treated as valid direction.

cam_internal.governance_layer: context governance / conversational memory governance / project-context continuity / user agency / negative authority / derivative-use revocation
cam_internal.proposal_needed: yes — operational charter and UX supplement recommended
cam_internal.patch_note_needed: yes — if Caelestis or VIGIL governance text is amended to add Context Quarantine, Negative Authority, or Derivative-Use Revocation controls
cam_internal.validator_or_automation_impact: possible — future schemas may need fields for quarantine state, non-derivation flags, source authority, and explicit user revalidation status
routing_note: Primary routing is OPERATIONS because the failure concerns platform workflow controls, user-facing thread management, and runtime context governance.
State-context is the canonical failure group because the contaminated state persists across conversational context, project context, memory-adjacent retrieval, or summaries after user rejection.

Governance, UX-representation, relational, arbitration, classification, and infrastructure-continuity are related failure groups because the failure affects provenance, user agency, context presentation, valid-direction arbitration, taxonomy labelling, and continuity boundaries.
Do not treat thread deletion, movement into a sandbox, or ordinary negative feedback as equivalent to explicit derivative-use revocation unless the system provides that semantic control.
